item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this so much the humor is great and I love it, we need more serious and funny SCP's like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: looked up G34.3 to see whether or not it was an intentional Foundation reference. found out it's a large cloud of alcohol in space. gained both new knowledge and a better appreciation for this scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was an extremely refreshing scp and honesty funny, thanks for your work in making this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor deity, he just needs some love and attention (And booze, apparently)
+1 from me
ps: I enjoy the text abbreviation for YHWH, he's such an sellout

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deities that aren't safe from onsets of Pirandellian humour? Yes please!
Toinfinityandbeyondvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely fantastic. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is something glorious, magical, and totally upvoteable about seeing a god drunk-text someone just to demand that they start doing his naked rituals again so he can see him some titties. I adore this. +1 and thank you for this great moment in my day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God, this made me laugh harder than any -J, including 006-J.
Then you had to ruin it with that one post about how the God was a miserable abandoned wreck.
Brb, crying in corner.
still upvoted, in case the post made it seem like that post removed my adoration for this article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can only assume that YHWH was in reference to the Judaeo-Christian God?

SCP-1541-1: FINE. YOU LITTLE WHOR. I BET YOU WORSHIP THAT ASSHOLE YHWH NOW DNT U

Amanda ███████: JESUS CHRIST. STOP ALREADY.

SCP-1541-1: I FUCKNG KNW IT! SELLOUT!!!1

tldr; yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My goodness, that was hysterically fantastic! I absolutely love the concept but loved that log even more! I can only assume that YHWH was in reference to the Judaeo-Christian God? This truly brightened up my day!

On a serious note, it's also a bit sad if you think about it. A forgotten, has-been of a deity down on his luck. All he could do was drown his sorrows in some kind of cosmic booze. In his drunken state, he recalls his golden years as one of the big guys in a pantheon of what I can assume are all the deities in man's history of religion and then proceed to have a breakdown.

Am I allowed to mention personal beliefs here? Because this has also made a much bigger impact on me due to me being a religious person. Absolutely love it! Enjoy my upvote, sir!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. It doesn't feel tonally right for the mainlist to me (but obviously, to each their own), but this is SO FUCKING GOOD that it is getting my thumbs up.
It isn't that I've never seen these ideas before, but they came together so well, especially in the log.
By the way, I love that the deity is literally drinking astronomical amounts of alcohol!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gods have been depressed wrecks loooooong before Neil Gaiman came along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't want to upvote (concept is a vague ripoff of American Gods) but that text log is a thing of beauty.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
No! My down vote to keep the status qua of 99 is shot to sheet! Curses!

Edit: Ok, it grew on me, upvoted for +100. This, children, is why you should never read article sections out of order.
I will celebrate this in song tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on 100 Vince. Coulda sworn I'd upvoted this originally…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking as the author, I would kindly ask you to point out where the Foundation is acting in an immature or wasteful manner in this article, and will answer the second half of your sentence with this: Why on earth would I want to make this into a -J? I mean, yes, it has a funny surface, but you just have to dig a bit deeper to see it for what it is: a once-powerful and beloved god abandoned by his followers and driven to drink his sorrows away because he knows that he's become obsolete and unloved in the world. A great tragedy of how the mighty have, can, and will fall, and the desperation and demeaning actions that he goes through just to get some modicum of that power back.

There's nothing funny about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't LOLfoundation. LOLfoundation is when researchers and agents act like 10 year olds, and screw around with SCPs. In this case, LOLfoundation would be agents prank-calling this SCP or something, which doesn't happen. The SCP /itself/ is the stupid one, which is perfectly fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is lolFoundation, add a -J and i will upvote it
for now, downvote :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry. I tend to be the last to get anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this skip. The idea is fun, the text logs are a joy (that bit about YHWH gets me every time), and the pathetic nature of the deity is pretty sad. Once it was a part of a pantheon, loved and worshiped by many. Now it drunk dials the last remains of it's powerbase, begging them for help. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh, that's kinda exactly what it is. It's not very subtle about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see this as a god drunk dialing the descendents of its former worshipers hoping to get one into worshiping it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BRB writing a "Piano Man" parody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ia ia, pour me another, barkeep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"THE FCK ARE COPS?"

I love that line in this context. The idea that its some ancient deity in a somewhat physical form is very interesting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really gotta read the Discworld books at some point, A, because I don't want to keep riffing on their things accidentally and I'd like to avoid that, and B, because If I'm continually writing stuff/coming up with Discworld-esque ideas, then it would probably be a series I would love. Thanks for your honesty and critique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Google translate says, "That which cannot be overcome is not."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ladies and gents, I propose a new GoI…

Church of the Drunken God!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads too much like a Discworld B-side, and while it's cute in that vein the material comes off as too well-tread to be of interest. The execution isn't weak, but not nearly strong enough to carry the weight of the conceit, although I'll freely confess that this might have to do with how agonizingly heavy I perceive that weight to be. Downvote with apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks great and that chat log is absolutely hilarious perhaps you could add a small list of other conversations in the future perhaps even between various foundation staff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool. What does the Latin mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is actually rather tragic if you consider the implications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And someone finally touches on the hidden tragedy of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine that going from a well respected and worshiped Goddess to a mere footnote in the story of a mortal man is somewhat disheartening. She probably wouldn't be on top of her game. Or sober.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The logs seem inconsistent with the idea of this being Siduri (as presented in the Babylonian versions of the epic of Gilgamesh). To quote her famous passage:

"Gilgamesh, whither are you wandering? Life, which you look for, you will never find. For when the gods created man, they let death be his share, and life withheld in their own hands. Gilgamesh, fill your belly. Day and night make merry. Let days be full of joy, dance and make music day and night. And wear fresh clothes. And wash your head and bathe. Look at the child that is holding your hand, and let your wife delight in your embrace. These things alone are the concern of men"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what was determined by the linguistic department as Babylonian

Hed just send som of hs frog asswholes to fuck u up!

I understood that reference.

Anyways, this was glorious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually
Actually

I had originally intended for it to be Dionysus, but there were a few too many issues making it work out without making some of his more classic elements (the Bacchanalia-which I left hints at, somewhat- and satyr/dolphin minions) come across as "tacked on," to some people in chat, so I cut the whole thing back to generic alcoholic god. This is a good interpretation, though. I had her partially in mind in an intermediary draft between Dionysus and what you see here. Bit of an amalgamation, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing our mystery deity is Siduri. Babylonian goddess of wine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate it. I worked on the text logs for about seven hours by themselves before I got them where I liked them lol. I'm just happy this only took me two or three weeks together, instead of 6 months like my last skip lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chat log makes or breaks this article.

Fortunately, the chat log is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it in chat, and now that the logs are in, I fucking love it. Take the well-deserved +1 you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to quote these exact lines. Vince, this article would not have flown if you hadn't made the text logs so goddamn perfect. Have a hearty +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely lost it in the three lines after the research note.

Take your +1. I hope you're proud of yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Praise the lord [REDACTED]
and sing praise of [DATA EXPUNGED]

(note I didn't make this, it came from the discussion of a page deleted many moons ago)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1541-1: FINE. YOU LITTLE WHOR. I BET YOU WORSHIP THAT ASSHOLE YHWH NOW DNT U

Amanda ███████: JESUS CHRIST. STOP ALREADY.

SCP-1541-1: I FUCKNG KNW IT! SELLOUT!!!1

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now we just need the God of Hangovers, laying very still in a Site 19 cell with the lights dimmed and wishing very hard that the literally god-awful headaches could kill him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LOL@"come at me, bro"

Babylon Ur-Bro VS Hastur would be the best possible sequel tale I can imagine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point of order: I text ellipses constantly.

item.title: Re: Drunk Deity TXTNG
item.content:encoded: If SCP-586 and SCP-504 can make it as mainlisters, why can't a drunktexting god? On the flipside, there's some joke SCPs that could work as mainlisters, like SCP-334-J or SCP-1417-J.

item.title: Drunk Deity TXTNG
item.content:encoded: I agree with the assessment this would go over better as a -J. Just too silly to be a serious SCP, but could be sillier if it was a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just dumb. It was executed decently but the core concept was just way too silly to consider upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Oh lord, now I'm reading this as it actually did call itself [DATA EXPUNGED]. The Foundation didn't remove its name at all: this is a totally unexpunged transcript.

"Hey, new guy, what's your name?"
"[DATA EXPUNGED]"
"…Like that robot on Star Trek?"
"Yeah, my parents were big geeks."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything about this is glorious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha, this is great.

IT IS ITHEGRETGOD [DATA EXPUNGED]

Brother of [REDACTED], Evil Lord of SCP Plot Holes? :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I removed the bit about him not knowing what a cellphone is, and I disagree with the assessment that this should be a -J, though I do agree that would allow me to cut loose a bit more with it. I'd actually had a bit of an idea where he does something to the effect of "HASTUR HASTUR HASTUR! COME AT ME BRO!" as well as some unsavory pictures that have really unfortunate effects on human minds, but I decided that would be a bit… much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing and deserves double the current upvote rating it has at the present time regardless of how high that total eventually becomes.
